title: PokéAPI Resource Identity, URL Construction, Pagination, and Fair Use · Failure Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Go Pokedex run contains one plausible command, parsing, URL, JSON, client, cache, ownership, goroutine, cancellation, persistence, security, test, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in fixed-origin PokéAPI resource and fair-use policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Keep scheme and authority fixed to the approved PokéAPI origin and map commands only to enumerated resource paths.
Distinguish resource type, numeric ID, canonical lowercase name, list query, and response self URL without unsafe equivalence.
Construct path segments and limit or offset queries with net/url while rejecting slash, control, and oversized input.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Pokedex Go Fixed Origin Policy
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 1805 during a disconnected community-lab recovery drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to admit fixed-origin resource URLs, bounded pagination, and fair-use request policy; no user-visible meaning may rely on color or cursor motion; then change one command, argument, EOF, or output-width condition and preserve the session contract. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep scheme and authority fixed to the approved PokéAPI origin and map commands only to enumerated resource paths.

Inspect the Pokedex Go Fixed Origin Policy trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: pokedex-go-fair-use-policy-19risd" that uses case 3533 to admit fixed-origin resource URLs, bounded pagination, and fair-use request policy. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact PokéAPI, read or write host files, handle real terminal or OS signals, spawn unbounded work, or claim race, leak, load, accessibility, cross-platform, package, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, race and fuzz runs, temporary files, subprocess and signal harnesses, inspected artifacts, representative platforms, accessibility review, capacity, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Pokedex Go Fair Use Policy
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Pokedex Go Fair Use Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug Go team handles case 7949 during a disconnected community-lab recovery drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to admit fixed-origin resource URLs, bounded pagination, and fair-use request policy; browser code must remain deterministic and side-effect-free; then inject one cache, waiter, goroutine, cancellation, or queue schedule and retain ownership evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Cache every requested resource, limit frequency and concurrency, identify the client responsibly, and avoid unsupported availability claims.
